title: ⚔️ Chapter boss ⭐⭐⭐: the tense of the past

questions.prompt: Complete: "Firas ___ his keys, so he waited outside for an hour."
questions.explanation: In the past negative, we use "didn't" and the verb goes back to its base form: didn't find. The common trap is "didn't found", which keeps the past form after "did" — but "did" already carries the past, so the main verb must be the base "find".

questions.prompt: Choose the correctly spelled past form: "The bus ___ at the station and we got on."
questions.explanation: "Stop" ends in one short vowel + one consonant, so we double the last consonant before -ed: stopped. "Stoped" forgets to double the p, and "stopd" and "stopize" are not real spellings.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct question about a place: (you went somewhere last summer)

options.text: Where you did go last summer?
Where did you went last summer?
Where did you go last summer?

questions.explanation: A WH-question in the past follows the order WH + did + subject + base verb: Where did you go? The common trap is "Where did you went", which keeps the past form after "did"; "Where you did go" and "Where you went" have the wrong word order.

questions.prompt: Which sentence puts "always" in the correct place?

options.text: When she was young, she read always before bed.
When she was young, she read before bed always.
When she was young, she always read before bed.
When she was young, always she read before bed.

questions.explanation: An adverb of frequency goes before the main verb: she always read before bed ✓ (this rule works in the past too). The common trap is putting it after the verb ("read always") or at the end ("before bed always"); "always she read" is not natural English.

questions.prompt: Read the text: "(1) Yesterday was a busy day. (2) I woke up early. (3) I eat a big breakfast. (4) Then I went to school." Which sentence contains a mistake?
questions.explanation: This item asks you to find the faulty sentence. The whole text is about "yesterday", so every verb must be past. Sentence 3 uses the present "eat" — it should be the past "ate": I ate a big breakfast ✓. Sentences 1, 2 and 4 correctly use "was", "woke" and "went".

questions.prompt: Complete: "How many books ___ she ___ last year?"
questions.explanation: "Last year" is a finished past time, so the question uses "did" + subject + base verb: did she read? The base of "read" is "read" (unchanged). The common trap is "did she readed" or "did she reads" — after "did", the verb is always the plain base; "does" would be present.
